message:
fix(types): non-numeric text arithmetic yields integer, not float (#81)

In C SQLite, `'hello' + 0` produces integer 0, not float 0.0.
Non-numeric text/blob (no numeric prefix) should use integer arithmetic
path, same as integer-prefix text. Added `is_float_numeric_type()` helper
that returns true only for text/blob with a numeric prefix containing
'.', 'e', or 'E'. Changed sql_add/sql_sub/sql_mul to use
`!is_float_numeric_type()` guard instead of `is_integer_numeric_type()`.

Also added 7 conformance oracle test suites: type affinity typeof,
window ranking (ROW_NUMBER/RANK/DENSE_RANK), window aggregates
(SUM/COUNT/AVG OVER), LAG/LEAD, NTILE, correlated WHERE subqueries,
index queries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
